steps.title: Recognize the structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: "confiscated ______ the private collections of French aristocrats." "From" is a preposition; "the private collections" is its object. Identify the rule: no punctuation may appear between a preposition and its object.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
No punctuation can appear between a preposition and its object. Test each choice:
  • Choice A — from;: "confiscated from; the private collections" — a semicolon joins two independent clauses, but "the private collections" is not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice B — from: "confiscated from the private collections" — no punctuation between preposition and object; grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice C — from:: "confiscated from: the private collections" — a colon requires a complete independent clause before it; "confiscated from" is not complete. ✗
  • Choice D — from,: "confiscated from, the private collections" — a comma cannot separate a preposition from its object. ✗
B is correct.
